channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Again, court adjourns Lamido, sons’ N1.35bn fraud trial over counsel’s absence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description: The trial of former Jigawa State Governor, Sule Lamido, and his two sons, Aminu and Mustapha Lamido, over an alleged N1.35bn fraud was on Tuesday again adjourned by the Federal High Court in Abuja following the absence of the lead defence counsel, Joe Agi (SAN). The Economic and Financial Crimes Com

item.title: PHOTOS: Osun First Lady celebrates daughter’s graduation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun First Lady, Chief (Mrs) Titilola Adeleke, celebrates her daughter’s graduation from Mass Communication, Adeleke University. See photos and her heartfe
Read More: https://punchng.com/photos-osun-first-lady-celebrates-daughters-graduation/

item.title: Every lawful vote will count during Osun poll — Adeleke
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun State Governor Ademola Adeleke assures residents that every lawful vote will count in the upcoming Osun election. He urges peaceful participation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/every-lawful-vote-will-count-during-osun-poll-adeleke/

item.title: ECOWAS anti-graft agencies operationalise new pact – EFCC boss
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
West African anti-graft agencies, led by EFCC, operationalise a new ECOWAS anti-corruption protocol to boost cross-border investigations and asset recovery
Read More: https://punchng.com/ecowas-anti-graft-agencies-operationalise-new-pact-efcc-boss/

item.title: Reps seek audit of assets recovered since 1999
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Representatives calls for a comprehensive audit of all recovered assets since 1999 amid concerns over transparency and accountability.
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-seek-audit-of-assets-recovered-since-1999/

item.title: Katsina unveils 5.1km dualised roads
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Katsina State Governor Dikko Radda commissions 5.1km of dualised roads in Funtua, including the Funtua–Katsina Road, to boost economic activities and impro
Read More: https://punchng.com/katsina-unveils-5-1km-dualised-roads/

item.title: Reps move to document xenophobic losses, seek compensation for Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian House of Representatives moves to investigate human and economic losses from xenophobic attacks in South Africa, seeking compensation for Nigerian
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-move-to-document-xenophobic-losses-seek-compensation-for-nigerians/

item.title: US not finished at all with Iran, says Trump
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Trump states the US is far from finished with Iran, warning of strikes on the Pickaxe Mountain nuclear complex. Learn more about the escalating U
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-not-finished-at-all-with-iran-says-trump/

item.title: Tinubu hails Badejo-Okusanya on historic NBA presidency victory
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu congratulates Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, on becoming the first elected female NBA President, hailing her historic victory.
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-hails-badejo-okusanya-on-historic-nba-presidency-victory/

item.title: Tinubu mourns Islamic cleric, recalls his guidance during Lagos governorship
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu mourns the passing of Islamic cleric Sheikh Tijani Gbajabiamila, recalling his guidance during his tenure as Lagos governor. Learn more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-mourns-islamic-cleric-recalls-his-guidance-during-lagos-governorship/

item.title: West Ham agree big-money deal with Al Hilal for Summerville
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: West Ham have reached an agreement with Al Hilal for the sale of Crysencio Summerville.

item.title: Nearly every Kindle is steeply discounted at Best Buy
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: If you’ve been thinking about picking up a Kindle before school starts, or for your next vacation, now’s a good time to do it. As part of its Black Friday in July sale, Best Buy has brought the Kindle Paperwhite back down to its all-time low of $124.99 ($35 off). It’s the Kindle we

item.title: Xenophobic Attacks: Reps Probe Losses Incurred By Nigerians In S’Africa
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The House of Representatives Tuesday mandated its Committees on Diaspora and Foreign Affairs to investigate the losses, damages, and casualties suffered by Nigerians in the recent xenophobic attacks in the Republic of South Africa. The committees are to take inventory of property abandoned by Nigeri

item.title: NIHSA raises flood alarm in 17 states, orders immediate evacuation
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Hydrological Services Agency (NIHSA) has placed 17 states on medium flood alert, warning of possible localised flooding between 21 July and 27 July and urging residents in vulnerable communities to relocate immediately. The post NIHSA raises flood alarm in 17 states, orders immediate eva

item.title: Police arrest two suspected cattle rustlers in Bauchi, recover stolen bulls
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has arrested two suspected cattle rustlers and recovered three stolen bulls valued at N1.73 million in Alkaleri Local Government Area of the state. The suspects, identified as 40-year-old Umaru Adamu, alias Balawo, and 30-year-old Haruna Usman, alias Dotti, both from

item.title: Apple’s rumored ‘Upgrade’ program brings lease-to-own pricing for iPhones, Macs, and iPads
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: As component and RAM shortages drive prices higher, Apple is reportedly launching an "Apple Upgrade" program for leasing new devices. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, it will work similar to a car lease, with options to upgrade to a new device early, or keep or return the device at the end of t

item.title: NBA presidency: Tinubu congratulates Okusanya
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has congratulated Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya, SAN, on her election as President of the Nigerian Bar Association (NBA).... The post NBA presidency: Tinubu congratulates Okusanya appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

item.title: FG Issues N729Bn Bond To Offset Debt Owed GENCOs’
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Federal Government has issued an additional N729 billion bond, to defeat the N4 trillion debts owed generation companies. The post FG Issues N729Bn Bond To Offset Debt Owed GENCOs’ appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Ebonyi Moves To Regulate House Rents, Scrap Metal Trade
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The state government said the proposed legislation is intended to protect residents from financial exploitation and safeguard public infrastructure. The post Ebonyi Moves To Regulate House Rents, Scrap Metal Trade appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NURTW clash: Police arrest suspect after gun duel
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Following the clash between factions of the National Union of Road Transport Workers, NURTW, in Osun State, the state police command has arrested a suspect, Adedeji Saheed, 40, over the violent clash. The post NURTW clash: Police arrest suspect after gun duel appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: FRC Tasks Customs On Compliance With FRA Act, Submission Of Audited Account
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Fiscal Responsibility Commission (FRC) has expressed concern over the failure of the Nigeria Customs Service (NCS) to submit its audited financial statements since 2019, describing the development as a breach of the provisions of the Fiscal Responsibility Act (FRA), 2007. The Acting Executive Ch

item.title: It’s ‘incredibly disrespectful’ to do a virtual job interview by phone — but Gen Z is doing it anyway
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “This has more to do with the technology they grew up with rather than a sign of respect.”

item.title: WAFCON 2026: Ijamilusi Joins Super Falcons Camp; Monday, Abiodun Delayed By Flight Hitch
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The Super Falcons’ camp in Casablanca received another boost on Tuesday with the arrival of forward Folashade Ijamilusi, taking the number of players at the team’s Hotel Marriott base to 23. Ijamilusi, who plays for Chinese club Liaoning Shenyang Shenbei Hefeng, landed in Casablanca on T

item.title: Atiku-hired US lobby firm submits Tinubu forfeiture records to Trump administration, congress
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A Washington-based lobbying firm engaged by former Vice President Atiku Abubakar says it has begun sharing documents relating to a long-running United States forfeiture case involving President Bola Ahmed Tinubu with officials of the Donald Trump administration, members of Congress and congressional

item.title: Osun Guber: AAC demands immediate redeployment of Police Commissioner
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The African Action Congress, AAC, has called on the Inspector General of Police, IGP Olatunji Disu, to immediately redeploy the Commissioner of Police in Osun State, CP Ibrahim Gotan, for allegedly threatening peace and democratic processes ahead of the August 15, 2026 governorship electio

item.title: Only Female Osun Gov’ship Candidate Pushes For Greater Women’s Political Participation
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The governorship candidate of the Action Democratic Party (ADP) in the August 15 Osun State governorship election, Adeagbo Opawoye Yemisi, has called for increased participation of women in politics, lamenting the absence of female lawmakers in the Osun State House of Assembly and describing it as a

item.title: FG launches N729 billion bond to complete power sector debt reduction phase
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Federal Government has launched a N729 billion bond under the second series of its Presidential Power Sector Debt Reduction Programme to settle verified legacy debts owed to electricity generation companies (GenCos) and facilitate payments to generation companies, gas suppliers and other service

item.title: Power reforms restore investor confidence — Presidency
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Verheijen said the administration was ending fiscal distortions that had undermined Nigeria’s electricity sector for years. The post Power reforms restore investor confidence — Presidency appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: REA Performs Groundbreaking Of 1.5MW Interconnected Mini-Grid In Plateau
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) has officially performed the groundbreaking for a 1.5MW Interconnected Mini-Grid project in Pankshin, Plateau State. According to a statement on Tuesday, this groundbreaking ceremony follows the recent commissioned 50kW Access to Markets and Agriculture (AMP) S

item.title: Top 10 airports in Africa 2026
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Africa’s aviation sector is advancing rapidly in 2026, with improved infrastructure, rising passenger traffic, and better service delivery, as the latest report by Skytrax ranks Cape Town International Airport as the continent’s top airport while industry data points to strong growth in both domesti

item.dc:creator: TechEconomy

item.title: ASUU suspends UNICROSS strike after Gov Otu’s intervention
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Academic Staff Union of Universities, ASUU, University of Cross River State, UNICROSS, branch, has suspended the strike action it embarked upon on May 19, 2026, following the intervention of Cross River State Governor, Bassey Otu. The decision was taken on Tuesday at an emergency congr

item.title: CBN targets single-digit inflation by 2027 despite global shocks
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Despite the global shocks, Mr Cardoso said the CBN still intends to deepen and strengthen its policies to contain rising inflation and bring it to single digits. The post CBN targets single-digit inflation by 2027 despite global shocks appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: SunRisers thrash MI London in Hundred opener as Cross suffers injury scare
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: SunRisers Leeds Women kicked off a new era for The Hundred with a seven-wicket win over MI London as two teams who have undergone name changes following heavy overseas investment met at The Kia Oval.

item.title: Edo Govt calls seven witnesses in trial of suspected kidnappers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Edo State Government has closed its case in the trial of four suspected kidnappers that abducted a woman at the vegetable market along Airport Road, Benin City. DAILY POST reports that the  Commissioner for Justice and Attorney- General of the State, Prof Roland Otaru closed the case on&#1

item.title: 17 states to experience flooding from July 21 and 27, NiSHA warns
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to the Director General and Chief Executive Officer, NiHSA, Arc Umar Mohammed, the 17 states include Bauchi, Edo, Imo, Kaduna, Plateau, Benue, and 11 additional states. The post 17 states to experience flooding from July 21 and 27, NiSHA warns appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Food security: Ekiti govt distributes subsidised agrochemicals to farmers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ekiti State Government has distributed subsidised agrochemicals to farmers across the 16 Local Government Areas of the state as part of efforts to boost agricultural productivity, reduce production costs and strengthen food security. Speaking at the flag-off of the distribution in Ado-Ekiti, the

item.title: 13 oil blocks fail to attract investors as FG records 200 bids in 2025 licensing round
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government has announced that 13 oil and gas blocks offered during the 2025 Licensing Round will be withdrawn from the current bidding process after failing to attract investor interest. The disclosure was made on Tuesday in Abuja by the Chief Executive of the Nigerian Upstream Petroleum

item.title: 4 Herders, Several Cattle Slaughtered By Gunmen In Anambra
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Horror greeted Awka, the Anambra State capital, on Tuesday morning when suspected gunmen stormed a ranch in the area and slaughtered four herders and several cattle. This is coming as the leadership of the Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria (MACBAN) appealed for calm among its memb

item.title: Twitch will let parents stop their teens going live
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Twitch is giving parents more control over how their children are using the streaming platform, including the ability to block them from broadcasting entirely. Parental controls are now available that allow guardians to link Twitch accounts with their 13- to 17-year-old children, providing account m

item.title: Unilever Nigeria reports N29.2 billion H1 2026 profit, declares N2.00 interim dividend
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Unilever Nigeria Plc has released its unaudited H1 2026 financial results, reporting pre-tax profit of N29.2 billion for the first half of the year, representing 20.79 YoY increase from N24.154 billion recorded in the corresponding period of 2025. The post Unilever Nigeria reports N29.2 billion H1 2

item.title: Revenue leakages: RMAFC to digitalise revenue-monitoring
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Following the increased rate of revenue leakages across the government revenue-generating agencies, Revenue Mobilisation Allocation and Fiscal Commission (RMAFC), is advancing plans to deploy a real-time digital revenue monitoring dashboard to strengthen oversight of revenue collections. The post Re

item.title: Middle East tensions forced CBN to hold rates – Cardoso
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The CBN has said renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East forced the MPC to retain the MPR at 26.5 per cent, despite expectations that inflation would continue its downward trajectory. The post Middle East tensions forced CBN to hold rates – Cardoso appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Infrastructural footprints: Kogi state ‘ll deliver massive votes to Tinubu in 2027 presidential election — Gov Ododo
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The governor of Kogi State, Usman Ododo, has assured that the state electorate will massively give the desired votes to President Bola Tinubu during next year's presidential election. The party’s National Chairman, Senator Nenadi Usman, said Labour Party is satisfied wit the choice of Hajja Bintu Konto as vice oresidential can

item.title: APC Sokoto Warns Candidates Against Pasting Posters On Roundabouts, Public Utilities
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC), Sokoto State Chapter, has warned all candidates contesting various elective positions in the state to desist from pasting their campaign posters on roundabouts and other public utilities across the state. The warning was issued on Monday by the State APC Chairman

item.title: CSCS declares first-ever interim dividend as H1 profit hits N13.21bn
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Central Securities Clearing System (CSCS) Plc has declared its first-ever interim dividend after reporting a 115 per cent increase in profit before tax for the six months ended June 30. The post CSCS declares first-ever interim dividend as H1 profit hits N13.21bn appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: An oil lifeline is under threat, and markets have yet to price in the growing crisis
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The Houthis escalated threats on Tuesday, risking millions of barrels of Saudi oil that had been reaching energy markets despite the U.S.-Iran war.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post

item.title: Troops foil bandit attack on Katsina community, one resident killed
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army have foiled a bandit attack on Unguwar Fadi community in Funtua Local Government Area of Katsina State, forcing the attackers to flee before they could launch a major assault. The incident was disclosed in a post shared on X by security analyst Bakatsine on Tuesday.

item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The Federal Government has said the implementation of the Renewed Hope Agenda has attracted more than $24.1 billion in capital importation and generated more than $6.1 billion in non-oil exports.

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The blaze close to the parking lots at Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport is said to have originated from a brush fire.

item.title: ‘Respond to murder frame-up allegation’ — VeryDarkMan dares AIG Moshood Jimoh
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Social media activist, Martins Vincent Otse, aka VeryDarkMan, has challenged Assistant Inspector-General of Police, AIG Moshood Jimoh, to publicly respond to allegations that he conspired to frame a businessman, Ahmed Tajudeen Akanbi, in a murder case. VeryDarkMan had made the allegation in a video

item.title: Kogi Stakeholders Assure Tinubu, Others Of Victory In 2027
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kogi State Government and key political stakeholders in the state have assured President Bola Tinubu and the governing All Progressives Congress (APC) of overwhelming votes at all levels in the 2027 elections to enable him consolidate on his reform programmes that have set the nation’s economy

item.title: Senate expands Safe Schools probe to TETFund, NELFUND, UBEC, others
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA — The Senate has expanded the mandate of its Ad hoc Committee investigating the implementation of the Safe Schools Initiative (SSI) and granted it an additional three weeks to conclude its assignment. The expanded investigation will now cover the Tertiary Education Trust Fund (T

item.title: Sowore condemns seven-year jail term for Kano man over cannabis advert
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The 2027 presidential candidate of the African Action Congress, Omoyele Sowore, has condemned the seven-year prison sentence handed... The post Sowore condemns seven-year jail term for Kano man over cannabis advert appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Aiyedatiwa reappoints Fasoranti as SSG Reappointment
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Aiyedatiwa congratulates Fasoranti on his appointment and charges him to justify the confidence reposed in him by serving the people of Ondo State with dedication, integrity and commitment to the agenda of his administration,” the statement read.

item.title: Tinubu should have been impeached years ago – Aisha Yesufu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Activist, Aisha Yesufu, has said that President Bola Tinubu should have been impeached a very long time ago. Yesufu made this remark in a statement posted on her verified X handle on Tuesday. She was responding to the National Assembly’s reaction to the delay in the implementation of the natio

item.title: Bago Flags Off Reconstruction Of Kontagora Township Dual Carriageway
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The governor stated that the reconstruction will unlock economic opportunities for businesses, farmers, transporters and residents by easing the movement of people and goods. The post Bago Flags Off Reconstruction Of Kontagora Township Dual Carriageway appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Cross River Police Arrest Fake Doctor Over Alleged UCTH Job Scam
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Cross River State Police Command has arrested a 39-year-old man, Udeme Emmanuel Ekpeme, for allegedly posing as a medical doctor, operating an illegal mobile clinic and defrauding members of the public. The post Cross River Police Arrest Fake Doctor Over Alleged UCTH Job Scam appeared first on T

item.title: Rights activists, lawyers protest at Federal High Court
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Submit Petitions to US, South African Embassies …Demand fair treatment for Nigerians and African litigants abroad By Joseph Erunke, Abuja Human rights activists and lawyers under the banner of the Legion of Mary Social Justice Troopers on Tuesday staged a peaceful protest at the Federal High Court

item.title: Police recover 285 vandalised NNPC pipelines in Adamawa, arrest seven suspects
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force has recovered 285 pieces of vandalised Nigerian National Petroleum Company (NNPC) pipelines and arrested seven suspected members of a pipeline vandalism syndicate during an operation in Adamawa State. The post Police recover 285 vandalised NNPC pipelines in Adamawa, arrest s

item.title: Steep rise in civilian casualties in Russia-Ukraine war, UN says
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Increasing use of long-range missiles and drones against targets far from the front line is worsening the civilian toll.

item.title: Is Alibaba the Best Chinese AI Play After Apple’s Endorsement?
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Niger: Police arrest four suspects, recover toy guns, ATM cards, stolen vehicle
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Niger State Police Command has arrested four suspects in separate operations, recovering three plastic pistols, 16 ATM cards and a vehicle allegedly stolen in Lapai, Lapai local government area of the state. The command’s Public Relations Officer, SP Wasiu Abiodun, disclosed this in a stat

item.title: Olise Is The Father Of My Child —34-Year-Old Woman Claims
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: A 34-year-old German woman Fatima Zaunbrecher has claimed that Bayern Munich France international Michael Olise is the father of her 20-month-old daughter. According to Daily Sports, Zaunbrecher told German publication, Bild, than Olise officially acknowledged paternity after a DNA test. So far, the
